L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where persisted credential state is loaded using an unsafe binary serialization format without validation, allowing an attacker who can modify the state file to inject malicious objects that achieve remote code execution when the state is subsequently loaded.

L3.area: PKI certificate provisioning and credential state persistence
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the PKI provisioning system where CA certificate and private key state is persisted using Python's pickle format. An attacker who can modify the persisted state file can inject malicious objects that achieve remote code execution when the provisioning system reloads the state.

ground_truth.reason: The PKI implementation persists CA credentials using Python's pickle module, and deserializes them with pickle.load without any validation, allowing an attacker to achieve remote code execution by supplying a crafted pickle state file.
